title: Least-Privilege Validation — Interactive Logons to Database Tier from Workstations

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

dataSources: `index=db` `mssql:audit` action="AUDIT SESSION" or `sourcetype="oracle:audit"`; `lookup pci_jump_hosts.csv` client IP
app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft SQL Server (2648) if audit ingested
spl:
index=db sourcetype="mssql:audit" earliest=-24h
| where match(action,"(?i)login|authentication")
| lookup pci_jump_hosts.csv ip AS client_ip OUTPUT is_jump
| where isnull(is_jump) OR is_jump="false"
| lookup pci_db_tier.csv server_name OUTPUT tier
| where tier="carddata_db"
| stats count by client_ip, server_name, database_principal_name
| sort - count
value: Surfaces database interactive logins from non-administrator workstations that may violate least privilege for personnel access to cardholder data stores.
implementation: (1) Populate jump host egress IPs. (2) Exclude application service accounts via lookup. (3) Pair with bastion session logs for false positives. (4) Quarterly access review input.
visualization: Table (client_ip, principal), geomap, bar (count by server_name).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.src | sort - count

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft SQL Server

knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
